data.title: Federal Reserve's Williams Highlights Increased Uncertainty and Risks
data.description: Federal Reserve's Williams has noted a rise in uncertainty and bidirectional risks. According to ChainCatcher, he stated that monetary policy is well-positioned to manage these challenges.

data.title: Fed's Williams Highlights Impact of Rising Energy Prices on Inflation
data.description: Federal Reserve's Williams has highlighted the impact of rising energy prices on the central bank's dual mandate. According to ChainCatcher, he noted that the increase in energy costs affects both inflation and disposable income. Williams also mentioned that the effects of the ongoing conflict on gasoline prices have not yet been reflected in the data.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Williams: Expected Hiring Scale to Decrease
data.description: Federal Reserve official John Williams has indicated that the anticipated scale of hiring is likely to decrease. According to Jin10, Williams highlighted concerns regarding employment trends, suggesting a potential slowdown in recruitment activities. This statement comes amid broader economic discussions about labor market dynamics and the implications for monetary policy. Williams' comments may influence market expectations and contribute to ongoing debates about the future direction of the U.S. economy.

data.title: AAVE Falls Below $95 to 52-Week Low Amid V4 Upgrade
data.description: AAVE has dropped below $95, reaching a 52-week low as Aave introduced its V4 upgrade this week. According to NS3.AI, the token has seen a decline of over a third in its value over the past year. The V4 upgrade replaces separate lending pools with a shared liquidity system, incorporates risk-based pricing, and aims to facilitate faster integration of new products and markets.

data.title: Lombard Finance Transfers Bitcoin to Anonymous Addresses
data.description: A total of 163.93 BTC was transferred from Lombard Finance to several anonymous addresses at 02:26, according to ChainCatcher. The transaction involved a significant amount of Bitcoin.

data.title: Bitcoin's 2% Decline Raises Concerns Over Dealer Gamma Exposure
data.description: Bitcoin has experienced a decline of approximately 2% over the past 24 hours, bringing its price down to $67,000. According to NS3.AI, this drop has moved the price below the $68,000 level, where dealer gamma exposure is predominantly negative. Glassnode reports that put positioning from $68,000 to the mid-$55,000 range could necessitate additional dealer hedging, potentially exacerbating losses. This situation is compounded by thin liquidity following the March 27 options expiry and the upcoming Easter holidays.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Advocates Spending Limits for AI-Connected Ethereum Wallets
data.description: Vitalik Buterin has proposed that AI tools linked to Ethereum wallets should have a daily autonomous spending limit of $100, with any transactions exceeding this amount requiring human approval. According to NS3.AI, Buterin emphasized the importance of privacy by running a private AI system on local hardware, thereby minimizing risks associated with cloud-based tools. He also noted that outbound messages from his system still necessitate manual approval.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Google Unveils Gemma 4 AI Lineup with High-Ranking Model
data.description: Google has introduced Gemma 4, a new lineup of open-weight AI models under the Apache 2.0 license. According to NS3.AI, the lineup includes four models, with the 31B Dense model achieving third place on Arena AI's open-model leaderboard. Google stated that these models are designed for a range of applications, including phones, edge devices, workstations, and cloud deployments.

data.title: JPMorgan Warns of Potential Oil Price Surge Amid Strait of Hormuz Disruptions
data.description: JPMorgan has issued a warning that oil prices could exceed $150 per barrel if disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz continue until mid-May. According to NS3.AI, the ongoing tensions in this critical shipping route could significantly impact global oil supply, leading to a substantial increase in prices. The Strait of Hormuz is a vital passage for oil transportation, and any prolonged disruption could have far-reaching consequences for the energy market.

data.title: Beep Expands Tradable Asset Range to Over 300
data.description: Beep has significantly increased its range of tradable assets from seven to more than 300. According to NS3.AI, this expansion includes support for cryptocurrencies, stocks, and commodities. The wallet's agent trader, R-2.5, facilitates these trades, ensuring they are executed on-chain and settled on Sui.

data.title: CFTC Files Lawsuits Against Arizona and Connecticut Over Prediction Markets
data.description: The U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) has initiated legal action against the states of Arizona and Connecticut concerning prediction markets. According to NS3.AI, the CFTC's lawsuits aim to reinforce its exclusive jurisdiction over these markets, following a similar case previously filed against Illinois. The agency's actions underscore its commitment to maintaining regulatory authority in the evolving landscape of prediction markets.

data.title: Iran Warns U.S. on Strait of Hormuz Amid Global Power Shift
data.description: Iranian official Sadegh Larijani has issued a warning to the United States regarding the Strait of Hormuz, stating that it will not revert to its pre-war condition. According to NS3.AI, Larijani emphasized that the regional dynamics have evolved beyond conventional warfare, entering a pivotal stage of global order transformation and power rebalancing. This statement reflects the ongoing geopolitical tensions and the strategic importance of the Strait of Hormuz in international trade and energy supply routes.

data.title: Investors Face Limited Exits as Private Credit Funds Cap Withdrawals
data.description:
Investors attempted to withdraw $13 billion from private credit funds this quarter, but received less than half of their requests due to withdrawal caps imposed by several major funds. According to NS3.AI, seven prominent firms, including Morgan Stanley, BlackRock, Apollo, Blue Owl, Cliffwater, Blackstone, and Ares, restricted investor exits during this period. Approximately $4.6 billion remains inaccessible across about a dozen funds.

Apollo introduced ACRED, a tokenized feeder into its Diversified Credit Fund. However, Securitize later removed advertised daily redemption rights after Unchained inquired about the discrepancy with the fund's 5% quarterly withdrawal limit.

data.title: UN Secretary-General Urges Halt to U.S., Israel, and Iran Conflicts
data.description:
UN Secretary-General António Guterres held a press conference at the United Nations headquarters in New York, addressing the ongoing conflicts involving the United States, Israel, and Iran. According to RTHK, Guterres called for the U.S. and Israel to cease attacks on Iran and for Iran to stop its aggression towards neighboring countries. He emphasized that the UN Security Council has condemned these attacks and reiterated the importance of respecting navigation rights and freedoms in critical maritime routes, including the Strait of Hormuz.

Guterres highlighted the severe human suffering caused by the conflict, noting its devastating economic consequences and the increasing scale of destruction. He warned that indiscriminate attacks are on the rise, placing the international community on the brink of a broader war.

The Secretary-General stressed the need for disputes to be resolved peacefully, respecting the sovereignty and territorial integrity of all member states. He also underscored the importance of protecting civilians and civilian infrastructure, including nuclear facilities, and maintaining freedom of navigation.

Guterres is actively engaging with all parties involved and has dispatched his envoy, Arnault, to the Middle East to seek a peaceful resolution.

data.title: SpaceX in Talks with Saudi Fund for $5 Billion Investment in 2026 IPO
data.description: SpaceX is reportedly in discussions with Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund regarding a cornerstone investment for its planned initial public offering (IPO) in 2026. According to Jin10, a Saudi fund is considering a $5 billion investment in SpaceX's IPO. This potential investment highlights the growing interest in SpaceX's future public offering and the strategic financial partnerships being formed ahead of the anticipated IPO.

data.title: Iranian Politician Uses Social Media to Address U.S. Audience Amid Conflict
data.description: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, an Iranian political figure, has started utilizing English-language posts on X to communicate directly with U.S. audiences during the ongoing conflict. According to NS3.AI, these messages focus on topics such as gas prices, economic challenges, and policy decisions made in Washington. While the account shows a 'connected via the US App Store' label, there is no definitive evidence that it is operated from the U.S. or by American individuals, as this label might be influenced by device settings or routing.

data.title: Solana Memecoin JONATHAN Surges 1,400% Following False Rumor of Tortoise's Death
data.description: Solana's memecoin JONATHAN experienced a dramatic surge of approximately 1,400% within an hour, reaching around $0.00038 on Wednesday. According to NS3.AI, this spike followed a false post on X claiming that Jonathan, a 193-year-old tortoise, had died. British Overseas Territories spokesperson Robert Midgley clarified that veterinarian Joe Hollins confirmed Jonathan is alive and that the X account spreading the rumor was fake.

data.title: OpenAI Acquires TBPN
data.description: OpenAI has reportedly acquired TBPN, according to Jin10. This acquisition marks a significant move in the AI industry, as OpenAI continues to expand its capabilities and resources. The details of the acquisition, including the financial terms and strategic implications, have not been disclosed. This development is expected to enhance OpenAI's technological offerings and strengthen its position in the competitive AI market.

data.title: IMF Highlights Impact of Tokenization on Financial Systems
data.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has stated that tokenization is significantly transforming the regulated financial system. According to NS3.AI, this development is influencing how financial institutions operate and interact with digital assets. The IMF's observations underscore the growing importance of tokenization in modern finance, as it continues to reshape traditional financial structures.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Brain-Inspired Chip Promises Significant Energy Efficiency in AI Tasks
data.description: Researchers at Loughborough University have developed a brain-inspired chip that could enhance energy efficiency in artificial intelligence tasks by up to 2,000 times. According to NS3.AI, the innovative device processes time-dependent data directly within its hardware, offering potential applications in fields such as weather tracking, biological signals, vehicles, and nuclear systems. The team believes this advancement could significantly reduce energy consumption in these areas, paving the way for more sustainable AI technologies.
